prompt: PCA was computed on the full 3K panel and then a set of 480 GBS samples were projected onto the existing PC axes (using --pca-allele-wts). Several projected GBS samples fall outside the convex hull of the WGS training samples. Interpret what this means and whether these projections are reliable.
context:
PCA projection of 480 GBS indica samples onto WGS-derived PC axes:

WGS indica cloud (n=1,306):
  PC1 range: [+0.08, +0.32]
  PC2 range: [-0.14, +0.12]

Projected GBS samples (n=480):
  N within WGS hull: 448 (93.3%)
  N outside WGS hull: 32 (6.7%)

Outlier characteristics:
  Mean DR2 of outliers: 0.71 (vs 0.84 for non-outliers)
  Mean missingness of outliers: 0.14 (vs 0.03 for non-outliers)
  Outlier PC1 range: [+0.04, +0.38]
  Outlier PC2 range: [-0.22, +0.18]

5 outliers have PC coordinates placing them closer to aus or tropical japonica clusters than to indica.
correct_answer: The 32 GBS samples projecting outside the WGS indica hull require careful evaluation: (1) IMPUTATION ARTIFACTS: The lower DR2 (0.71 vs 0.84) and higher missingness (0.14 vs 0.03) of outliers suggests imputation errors are distorting their projected PC coordinates. PCA projection uses genotype values at the WGS-derived loading SNPs -- if many of these are poorly imputed in the GBS samples, the projected coordinates will be noisy and can fall outside the true population range. This is the most likely explanation for most of the 32 outliers. (2) TRUE BIOLOGICAL OUTLIERS: The 5 samples projecting near aus or tropical japonica may be genuinely misclassified in the GBS panel metadata -- they could be aus-indica admixed landraces or indica accessions with japonica introgressions. These should be verified against passport data. (3) PROJECTION RELIABILITY: PCA projection is generally reliable when the projected samples are genotyped at the same SNPs with comparable quality. For imputed GBS data, projection accuracy degrades because: (a) imputation errors are correlated across nearby SNPs (from haplotype template sharing), creating systematic biases in PC coordinates, (b) GBS covers only a subset of the SNPs used for PC loading calculation, requiring imputation for the rest. RECOMMENDATION: Filter outliers by DR2 (require mean DR2>0.80 across PC loading SNPs) and verify the 5 subpopulation-discordant samples against independent data (e.g., diagnostic SNP panels). Do not simply exclude all outliers, as some may represent genuine biological diversity not captured in the WGS panel.
rubric: Full marks (3): Must identify imputation quality as the primary cause of most outliers, distinguish true biological outliers (the 5 subpopulation-discordant samples), explain why PCA projection degrades with imputed data, and provide actionable recommendations. Mostly correct (2): Identifies imputation as cause but misses the biological outlier distinction or practical recommendations. Partial (1): Notes outliers exist without diagnosing the cause. Incorrect (0): Claims all outliers are biological or all are artifacts.
notes: PCA projection of imputed data onto WGS-derived axes is common practice but has known quality issues.
